escalatedRisks.risk: Micron CEO: tight supply beyond 2027 — major suppliers fully booked 🆕
escalatedRisks.status: SUSTAINED — Day 2. Highest-level executive confirmation of extended supply constraint.
escalatedRisks.detail: Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra reiterates DRAM/NAND supply will remain tight through 2027 and beyond. Major memory suppliers already fully booked. Confirms structural undersupply independent of Samsung union situation. Aligns with Gartner +125% DRAM 2026 forecast and Goldman Sachs NAND 4.2% deficit.
escalatedRisks.confidence: high (CEO-level statement, consistent with all other macro indicators)

escalatedRisks.risk: Goldman Sachs global NAND 4.2% deficit (largest since 2011)
escalatedRisks.status: SUSTAINED — Day 3. Highest-level independent NAND supply quantification.
escalatedRisks.detail: Global NAND flash deficit of 4.2% forecast for 2026 — largest since 2011. NAND Q2 contract +70-75% QoQ (largest quarterly increase in a decade). Enterprise SSD 53.8% of market. Combined with full-chain sold-out, Kioxia 2026 sold out, Samsung warm-down production cut, and MLC NAND tripled.

escalatedRisks.risk: Samsung PM9D3a 30.72TB PCIe5 reference mixed signals ⚠️
escalatedRisks.status: MIXED SIGNALS — Day 7. UPGRADED: 30.72TB capacity/yield bottleneck (15.36TB model ships 4-6 days normally).
escalatedRisks.source: Compuram / Xi Computer / SHI / Neobits May 2026
escalatedRisks.detail: Samsung PM9D3a 30.72TB listed as 'currently not deliverable' on Compuram. However, PM9D3a 15.36TB model ships 4-6 days normally — implying 30.72TB bottleneck is capacity/yield issue, not product-line failure. Xi Computer $14,239 still listed. If ultimately undeliverable, 4-brand landscape degrades to 3-brand.

escalatedRisks.risk: Former Samsung exec predicts DDR5 price fall H2 2027 from China expansion
escalatedRisks.status: SUSTAINED — Day 3. Corroborated by Corsair/CXMT supply diversification + ServerMonkey May 2026.
escalatedRisks.source: Kye Kyung-hyun speech at National Academy of Engineering Seoul May 2026; Corsair/CXMT May 2026
escalatedRisks.detail: Former Samsung semiconductor chief predicts DDR5 prices could fall significantly H2 2027-early 2028 from Chinese DRAM expansion. Now independently corroborated by Corsair/CXMT sourcing decision. China 'Epic Expansion' nearly doubling wafer output. First credible timeline for price normalization — but 14-18 months out.
categoriesAffected: All DDR5 (consumer tier primary, enterprise RDIMM lagged)

escalatedRisks.risk: Samsung semiconductor strike
escalatedRisks.status: AVERTED with CAVEAT — tentative deal reached. Vote Day 4 SGUU 86.2%. DX Donghaeng union excluded+legal escalation. Deadline May 27 (48hr).
escalatedRisks.detail: Samsung and NSEU tentative wage deal reached 5/20. Union vote started 5/22 (69.15% Day 1 → 79.42% Day 2). Deadline May 27. DX division (mobile/TV/appliances) strongly opposing deal (100:1 bonus gap). Court injunction + shareholder lawsuit + 10K-12K new anti-deal union members. Production cut since 5/14 (360K wafers) FACT regardless of vote outcome — warm-down→ramp-up recovery 2-3 weeks.

escalatedRisks.risk: DX Donghaeng Union formally excluded from Samsung vote — legal escalation 🆕
escalatedRisks.status: NEW — Day 1. DX union excluded from official vote. Legal challenge may invalidate ratification.
escalatedRisks.source: Korea JoongAng Daily / Chosun Ilbo / Seoul Economic Daily May 24-25 2026
escalatedRisks.detail: DX Donghaeng union (10K-12K DX employees) formally excluded from Samsung vote by SGUU main union. Exclusion may violate Korean labor law — legal challenge launched. Court hearing held May 20. If successful, entire ratification could be invalidated regardless of vote outcome. Deepens DS ($335K-$400K bonus) vs DX ($4K-$34K) institutional rift. Operational risk extends beyond May 27 deadline.

escalatedRisks.risk: NAND Research May 2026 'extreme seller's market' — system-wide escalation 🆕
escalatedRisks.status: NEW — Day 1. NAND wafer +246% vs Q1 2025. Market shifted to allocation-driven model.
escalatedRisks.detail: NAND Research characterizes market as 'extreme seller's market' with no credible near-term relief. NAND wafer pricing +246% vs Q1 2025. Market shifted from consumer-electronics cycle to allocation-driven market — hyperscalers compete for limited wafer/SSD capacity. New fab capacity not reaching meaningful volume before late 2027-2028. Procurement teams advised to build memory-cost escalation models. Consistent with Goldman Sachs 4.2% deficit, Gartner +125% DRAM, NAND Q2 +70-75%.
escalatedRisks.confidence: high (independent industry research, consistent with all other macro indicators)
